channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How weight loss drugs are reshaping supermarket trends
item.description: The rise of Ozempic and similar GLP-1 medications, initially developed for diabetes, has transformed Australian supermarket shopping over the past five years. These drugs, including Wegovy and Mounjar...

item.title: Supreme Court Grants Bail To Shabir Shah Pending Terror Funding Trial
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has granted bail to Shabir Shah, a well-known separatist leader from Jammu and Kashmir, in connection with an ongoing terror funding case. Shah has been under judicial custo...

item.title: Nyeri Graduate Seeks Public Service Job to Apply Social Work Skills
item.description: Leah Wanjiru Mwangi, a young graduate from Nyeri County, has gone public with her plea for employment in public service. Armed with a Diploma in Social Work and Community Development, Leah has been un...

item.title: Proposed ACT federal electorate boundary changes to affect 1 in 10 voters
item.description: The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) has released proposed new federal electorate boundaries for the ACT, which would see around 10% of voters moved between the Canberra, Bean, and Fenner elector...

item.title: Senate Sub-Committee Directs FBR and FIA to Investigate Rs250m Cigarette Theft from Swabi and Mardan Godowns
item.description: A sub-committee of the Senate Standing Committee on Interior and Narcotics Control, chaired by Senator Saifullah Abro, has instructed the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) and relevant investigation agen...

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2026: Key Announcements and AI Developments to Watch
item.description: Nvidia’s annual GPU Technology Conference (GTC) 2026 will begin next week in San Jose, California, with CEO Jensen Huang delivering the keynote on Monday at 11 a.m. PT / 2 p.m. ET. The keynote will hi...

item.title: Porto Take First Leg Advantage Over Stuttgart With Moffi’s Goal
item.description: Porto don put one foot for Europa League quarter-finals after dem collect 2-1 win against VfB Stuttgart for first leg of Round of 16. Nigerian forward Terem Moffi shine well well, scoring the opening ...

item.title: Exploring Heritage, Migration, and Connection in Jake Hoddinott’s Novel
item.description: Jake Hoddinott’s 'Migrations Beneath a Baobab' is a deeply immersive novel that brings South African history and landscape to life. The book explores themes of heritage, displacement, and resilience, ...

item.title: National Assembly halts Frontier Constabulary reorganisation bill amid quorum issue
item.description: In a significant setback for the government, the National Assembly on Thursday failed to pass the Frontier Constabulary Reorganisation Bill due to lack of quorum, as opposition members actively disrup...

item.title: Opposition questions government response after Iranian missile strikes Canadian base in Kuwait
item.description: The Canadian Opposition Conservatives have raised concerns over a delay in public disclosure following an Iranian missile strike on the Canadian section of the Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait, also kn...

item.title: Kyle Echarri expresses pride in his challenging role in ABS-CBN series 'Roja'
item.description: Kyle Echarri, the Kapamilya actor and singer, recently shared his feelings about his performance in the ABS-CBN action-drama series 'Roja,' which concluded on March 13, 2026. In an interview, he expre...

item.title: Marquise Williams Will Skip UNC Games Amid Controversy Surrounding Bill Belichick
item.description: Marquise Williams, a former All-ACC quarterback at the University of North Carolina, has announced that he will not attend any Tar Heels football games during the 2026 season. The decision comes as UN...

item.title: UK Defence Secretary John Healey announces consideration of military options to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict
item.description: Defence Secretary John Healey has revealed that the United Kingdom is actively considering ways to assist in defending commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global ...

item.title: Master Wong Arrested Following Robbery Allegations in Kiamaiko
item.description: Online personality Master Wong has been arrested after allegations of robbery surfaced, brought forward by musician Toxic Lyrikali and his associate Mauru Gwash. According to reports, a confrontation ...

item.title: Five Men Face Court Again in Senzo Meyiwa Murder Trial
item.description: The trial of the five men accused of murdering South African soccer star Senzo Meyiwa continues at the Pretoria High Court. Meyiwa, who was the captain of Bafana Bafana, was tragically killed more tha...

item.title: Barrister Adeleke Adeyemo marks 80th birthday by donating church to Iragbiji community
item.description: For Iragbiji, Osun State, na serious celebration happen as eminent lawyer Barrister Adeleke Adeyemo mark him 80th birthday. Him family, friends, colleagues, and community people gather gidigba for him...

item.title: Supreme Court allows passive euthanasia in permanent vegetative state cases
item.description: In a landmark decision, the Supreme Court of India has for the first time permitted passive euthanasia for a patient in a permanent vegetative state, following the case of Harish Rana, who had been co...

item.title: Ontario court dismisses lawsuit targeting The Globe over investigative reporting on alleged foreign interference
item.description: An Ontario Superior Court judge has dismissed a $10-million lawsuit filed by former Ontario Liberal cabinet minister Michael Chan against The Globe and Mail. Chan claimed punitive and personal damages...

item.title: First Nations leaders reaffirm Treaty 6 and Canada's unity at Buckingham Palace
item.description: On March 12, 2026, a delegation of Treaty 6 First Nations chiefs from Alberta and Saskatchewan met King Charles at Buckingham Palace. The meeting coincided with the upcoming 150th anniversary of the T...

item.title: Delhi Introduces GPS-Equipped Tankers to Monitor Water Distribution
item.description: The Delhi government has introduced a new system of GPS-tracked water tankers to improve transparency and efficiency in the supply of water across the city. This initiative aims to tackle the long-sta...
